name: crud
context: Read and write MongoDB documents with filters, projections, updates, deletes, and bulk operations
info: This skill covers the core document lifecycle through the official Node.js driver: querying with filters and cursors, projecting fields, sorting and paginating results, inserting one or many documents, using update operators or full replacements, deleting selectively, and batching operations with `bulkWrite()`. Agents use it for application data changes, audits, migrations, and repair jobs. Single-document writes are atomic, while broader transactional guarantees live in [[mongodb/transactions]].
body:
# MongoDB CRUD

Use this skill for the normal document lifecycle. It maps directly onto the official Node.js driver and gives you structured automation instead of shell-only snippets.

## Sub-Skills

- **[[mongodb/crud/find]]** — focused read patterns with filters, projections, sort, skip, and limit
- **[[mongodb/crud/write]]** — focused insert, update, replace, delete, and bulk write patterns

## Read Example

```javascript
const docs = await db.collection("users")
  .find({ active: true }, { projection: { email: 1, role: 1 } })
  .sort({ createdAt: -1 })
  .limit(20)
  .toArray();
```

## Write Example

```javascript
await db.collection("users").updateOne(
  { email: "ops@example.com" },
  { $set: { role: "admin" }, $currentDate: { updatedAt: true } },
  { upsert: true }
);
```

## Bulk Example

```javascript
await db.collection("users").bulkWrite([
  { updateOne: { filter: { email: "a@example.com" }, update: { $set: { active: true } } } },
  { deleteOne: { filter: { email: "legacy@example.com" } } }
], { ordered: true });
```

A `find()` call returns a cursor, not an array. Convert it with `toArray()` only when the result set is bounded. For large scans, iterate the cursor or add tighter filters. For multi-document atomicity or cross-collection changes, route to [[mongodb/transactions]].

sections.title: Read patterns
sections.body: Use filters to reduce the result set, projections to cut payload size, and sort/skip/limit for deterministic pagination or sampling. `findOne()` is a convenience form for a single result and behaves like a limited query. See [[mongodb/crud/find]] for a focused version of these patterns.

sections.title: Write patterns
sections.body: Choose `insertOne()` or `insertMany()` for new documents, `updateOne()` or `updateMany()` for operator-based changes, `replaceOne()` when replacing an entire document shape, and `deleteOne()` or `deleteMany()` for removals. Use `bulkWrite()` when you need a single batched round trip with ordered or unordered semantics.

sections.title: Atomicity and escalation
sections.body: A single-document write is atomic. That is often enough if related state is embedded together. Escalate to [[mongodb/transactions]] only when a change truly spans multiple documents or collections and cannot be modeled safely as a single-document update.

links.context: Connect with the correct URI and role before any CRUD work
Focused read workflow for queries and cursors
Focused write workflow for inserts, updates, deletes, and bulk operations
Escalate to transactions when the write spans multiple documents or collections
Keep application writes aligned with server-side validators

related.note: CRUD operations fail immediately when the connection string, roles, or topology are wrong
Use transactions only when single-document atomicity is not sufficient

scripts.name: Run CRUD operation
scripts.description: Execute a structured CRUD operation through the MongoDB Node.js driver using Extended JSON input

CRUD_INPUT_JSON.description: Extended JSON payload. If omitted, the script reads JSON from stdin.

filter.description: Query filter for reads, updates, deletes, or counts

document.description: Single document payload for insertOne or replaceOne

options.description: Driver options such as projection, sort, limit, upsert, ordered, hint, or write concern

acknowledged.description: Whether the server acknowledged the write

documents.description: Read result set for find operations

count.description: Count returned by countDocuments or number of returned documents for find

depends.reason: CRUD automation needs a valid connection string, reachable deployment, and adequate roles

author: dojo-community
